items.title: Neale Daniher, former AFL player and motor neurone disease campaigner, dies aged 65

children.content: Former Essendon player and Demons coach Neale Daniher has died, aged 65. He was diagnosed with motor neurone disease in 2013 and campaigned to raise awareness of the disease and increase research.

articles.title: Neale Daniher to be remembered with state funeral, premier reveals

children.content: A great of the game and icon after his retirement, Neale Daniher is being remembered as a fighter and an inspiration after his death.

image.altText: Neale Daniher sits in a wheelchair front of Melbourne Demons fans.

gallery.altText: An older man wears a blue beanie with a logo saying "Big Freeze 2: Cure for MND Foundation".

thumbnail.altText: An older man wears a blue beanie with a logo saying "Big Freeze 2: Cure for MND Foundation".

items.title: Paul Brereton resigns from National Anti-Corruption Commission

children.content: National Anti-Corruption Commission chief Paul Brereton has resigned, three years into his term. Follow live.

image.altText: Paul Brereton speaks during a committee hearing.

items.title: Government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to regional airports

children.content: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.

image.altText: The outside of an aircraft printed with the letters REX in blue and orange

items.title: Dezi Freeman goaded officers to 'come and get me' before fatal shooting

children.content: The Coroners Court has heard about Dezi Freeman's final moments before a fatal confrontation at his hideout in Victoria's north-east.

image.altText: Screengrab from footage of Dezi Freeman wearing sunglasses and gesturing while speaking with police.

items.title: Trump says a deal is close. Here are the sticking points still dividing the US and Iran

children.content: A possible US-Iran agreement is taking shape, but the toughest issues are still unsettled, including reopening the Strait of Hormuz, curbing Tehran's nuclear ambitions, and ending regional fighting.

image.altText: A photo of a shipping vessel carrying cargo containers in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: 'I thought the plane was going down': 10 injured on Cathay Pacific flight

children.content: An Australian passenger aboard a flight from Brisbane to Hong Kong says he thought he was going to die after the aircraft suddenly plunged mid-air.

items.title: V'landys to take over as NRL CEO after Abdo quits to join Tennis Australia

children.content: NRL CEO Andrew Abdo causes a shock in the rugby league world by departing mid-season after a highly-successful six-year stint.

image.altText: Andrew Abdo

items.title: Magnussen flops at Enhanced Games, finishes last twice

children.content: James Magnussen, wearing a garish golden suit, has finished dead last at the inaugural Enhanced Games event.

image.altText: James Magnussen, shirtless, smiles beside a pool with competitors behind him at the Enhanced Games.

items.title: 'Surprise attack' left 11 shot within seconds of Bondi massacre starting

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has entered a critical phase, assessing the circumstances surrounding the Bondi terror attack with ASIO director-general Mike Burgess defended counter-terrorism resourcing

items.title: Man, 23, charged with murder over death of eight-week-old baby

children.content: A 23-year-old man has been charged with murder over the death of an eight-week-old child in suburban Perth.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Victim of fatal Queensland shark attack identified

children.content: While the Far North Queensland community is reeling from the death of local spearfisher Michael Jensz, experts warn human-shark interactions are increasing.

image.altText: a small pontoon on a river

items.title: China launches astronauts into space amid new race to Moon

children.content: China has launched three astronauts into space, including one who will stay for a year for a study of the long-term impacts on a human in orbit, ahead of Beijing's goal of a crewed Moon landing by 2030.

image.altText: China's Shenzhou-23 takes off with fire and light

items.title: How a container of hot chocolate put the spotlight on airport security

children.content: A security scare that shut down a Victorian airport last week was found to be a false alarm.

image.altText: Passengers line up for security screening at the airport.

items.title: Man jailed for fatally amputating man's foot using circular saw

children.content: Kalman Tal's loved ones have told of their distress at how the 66-year-old's leg was amputated in a public park. Farm worker John Yalu has today been sentenced for manslaughter.

image.altText: A selfie image of a man wearing a black top on a tropical farm

items.title: Dog-friendly cafe issued warning after customer complaint

children.content: Kellie Hunter opened her cafe in the Adelaide Hills in 2023 as a friendly place for dogs and their owners, but the local council has now put a stop to it after one complaint.

items.title: Notorious Perth paedophile Bradley Pen Dragon released from jail again

children.content: A notorious Perth paedophile who breached his prison release conditions 11 times has again been freed into the community, as the child protection minister argues it may be unconstitutional to keep him in custody.

image.altText: onvicted Australian paedophile Bradley Pendragon

items.title: Push for law change that could keep Baden-Clay behind bars for longer

children.content: The possible release of wife killer Gerard Baden-Clay next year could be pushed back by up to a decade under proposed changes to Queensland's parole laws.

image.altText: Gerard Baden-Clay

items.title: 'I did it': Parramatta council official fronts corruption watchdog

children.content: During her third day on the witness stand, Roxanne Thornton told the Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) she was responsible for hiring processes that were "extremely flawed".

image.altText: Roxanne Thornton in a black blazer, purple scarf, green glasses and short cropped ash blonde hair, speaking.

items.title: Aboriginal community workers say diphtheria response 'heartbreaking'

children.content: Residents and service providers in the remote community of Yuendumu, where there are reports of dozens of diphtheria cases, say the NT government has not done enough to keep people safe.

image.altText: a cow standing near a fence with a hill in the background

items.title: Australia's opening times are world leading and researchers say it's a strength

children.content: Australia has a "distinctive strength" when it comes to early trading, but more knowledge is needed for businesses to fully capitalise on it.

items.title: Committee rejects calls to review greyhound phase-out bill

children.content: A parliamentary committee has rejected a request to review a bill which would phase out Tasmania's greyhound racing industry. It follows new data which shows 19 ex-racing greyhounds have died in Tasmania this year.

image.altText: Greyhounds racing.

items.title: Surfing event in NZ issued 'code red' after animal attacks photographer

children.content: The New Zealand Pro World Surf League is put on hold after what is believed to be a seal attacked a photographer.

image.altText: A surfer is taken out of the water by a jet ski.

items.title: Housing minister says Waterloo community 'not functional' as demolition looms

children.content: Rose Jackson made the comment on radio as workers began fencing off 150 public homes to be demolished to make way for a mixed-tenure development.

items.title: Travis Head and wife Jess cop social media abuse after IPL on-field incident

children.content: Australian cricketer Travis Head and his wife Jess Head have their Instagram accounts blasted with vile comments after a series of tense on-field exchanges between Head and Indian star Virat Kohli in an Indian Premier League match.

image.altText: Australian batsman Travis head looks at his bat while Indian fielder Virat Kohli smiles and shouts.

items.title: McLaren 'looked like idiots', Brit feels fate is ruining his F1 title dream

children.content: McLaren has "looked like idiots" after a bold tyre choice at the start of the Canadian Grand Prix backfired immediately for its two drivers.

items.title: Turnbull says party of independents could fill 'vacuum' in politics

children.content: Independent MPs Zali Steggall, Allegra Spender and David Pocock have confirmed they are in conversations about a potential party of independents in response to the rising popularity of One Nation.

items.title: Gold medallist who endured 16 surgeries in 9 months hangs up oars

children.content: Paralympic gold medallist Nikki Ayers reflects on a career that saw her face ableism, homophobia and sexism.
